_comment: Erenumab change-from-baseline in monthly migraine days (MMD) dose-response in episodic migraine, 3 Phase 3 RCTs (STRIVE, ARISE, LIBERTY). Per-arm LSM/MEAN + SE extracted from AACT snapshot 2026-04-12 (outcome_measurements.txt + outcome_counts.txt). CROSS-TRIAL OUTCOME SELECTION: only STRIVE tests 2 doses (70 mg + 140 mg); ARISE tests 70 mg only; LIBERTY tests 140 mg only. STRIVE primary is Δ MMD (months 4-6); ARISE primary is Δ MMD (Week 12); LIBERTY primary is the binary >=50% responder rate at week 12 with Δ MMD as a SECONDARY endpoint — we pool on Δ MMD because (a) it is primary in 2 of 3 trials, (b) it preserves more information than a dichotomized responder rate, and (c) the same endpoint exists in all 3 AACT registrations. Effective k=3 trials with mixed dose coverage: each trial contributes >=1 contrast versus placebo, and the engine's GL covariance handles per-trial contrast counts independently. ALL three PMIDs were verified via PubMed MCP get_article_metadata on 2026-05-14; user-supplied citations: STRIVE (Goadsby 2017 NEJM 377:2123-32) and ARISE (Dodick 2018 Cephalalgia 38:1026-37) were correct. LIBERTY: user-supplied citation 'Reuter U et al. Lancet 2018;392(10161):2280-2287' is correct, but the implied PMID lookup was a single-record-off mistake — PMID 30360966 is a Lancet kinase-inhibition comment, NOT LIBERTY. The correct LIBERTY PMID is 30360965 (verified via search 'LIBERTY erenumab Reuter Lancet 2018' returning a unique hit). Erratum documented; the abstracts fixture uses PMID 30360965. POPULATION HETEROGENEITY CAVEAT: LIBERTY enrolled patients with PRIOR FAILURE of 2-4 preventive treatments (treatment-refractory population) while STRIVE and ARISE enrolled treatment-naive episodic migraine patients. This contributes to between-trial heterogeneity in the dose-response (LIBERTY's placebo-arm Δ MMD is only -0.16 vs -1.83/-1.84 in STRIVE/ARISE — much smaller placebo response in refractory patients) but does NOT violate dose-equivalence (same molecule, same route, same SC injection schedule, same 12-week double-blind window for ARISE+LIBERTY, 6-month DB for STRIVE). LIBERTY's dispersion is reported as MEAN ± SE (NOT LSM); the AACT secondary outcome row uses param_type=MEAN. STRIVE and ARISE use LSM ± SE. The engine input 'sd' field is computed via sd = se * sqrt(n) for all 3 trials with _aact_se preserved as the raw SE for audit. Engine mdCovariance() uses sd^2/n = se^2 which is invariant to MEAN-vs-LSM re-expression at the variance level. PER-TRIAL MONOTONICITY (2026-05-14): (1) STRIVE: pbo -1.83 -> 70mg -3.23 -> 140mg -3.67 STRICTLY MONOTONIC; (2) ARISE: pbo -1.84 -> 70mg -2.88 STRICTLY MONOTONIC (only one contrast); (3) LIBERTY: pbo -0.16 -> 140mg -1.75 STRICTLY MONOTONIC (only one contrast). All 3 trials show MORE negative Δ MMD at higher erenumab dose — strictly monotonic across all 4 within-trial contrasts. Expected pooled linear slope on log-md scale is negative (more reduction per mg).
source: Pooled erenumab Δ monthly migraine days dose-response, 3 Phase 3 trials (AACT 2026-04-12)
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial dose (70 mg or 140 mg erenumab subcutaneous once monthly; placebo arms encoded as dose=0). STRIVE used QM dosing for 6 months; ARISE and LIBERTY used QM dosing for 12 weeks (double-blind phase).
class_equivalence_caveat: All 3 trials use the SAME molecule (erenumab, AMG334, a human IgG2 mAb against the CGRP receptor) and SAME route (subcutaneous once-monthly injection); pooled slope is a within-drug Δ MMD dose-response curve. Trials differ in (a) double-blind duration (STRIVE 24 weeks; ARISE 12 weeks; LIBERTY 12 weeks) — STRIVE's primary endpoint is the average across months 4-6 while ARISE/LIBERTY use the last 4 weeks of a 12-week treatment phase, (b) population (STRIVE/ARISE: treatment-naive episodic migraine; LIBERTY: prior failure of 2-4 preventives, treatment-refractory), and (c) dose coverage (STRIVE: pbo+70+140; ARISE: pbo+70; LIBERTY: pbo+140). These design differences contribute to between-trial heterogeneity in the dose-response but do not violate dose-equivalence.
active_comparator_handling: Not applicable — all 3 trials are placebo-controlled with no active comparator arms. Placebo is the dose=0 reference in all 3 trials. (Unlike SUSTAIN/SURPASS/SELECT/AMAGINE fixtures, no Option A active-comparator drop is required here.)
estimand_uniformity: Treatment-policy / FDA-pre-registered primary estimand (MMRM analysis with multiple imputation per protocol-specified statistical analysis plan, treatment-policy population) used consistently across all 3 trials. AACT registers a single estimand row per result_group per outcome_id in all 3 trials (no on-treatment-vs-in-trial split like SUSTAIN-FORTE). For STRIVE and ARISE, AACT param_type=LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN reflects the MMRM-based primary analysis. For LIBERTY, AACT param_type=MEAN reflects the secondary Δ MMD report (the primary on the >=50% responder rate is binary; MEAN on the secondary is the same MMRM-derived estimate, just labelled MEAN in AACT registration).
dispersion_handling: All 3 trials report ± Standard Error in AACT: STRIVE and ARISE as LSM ± SE (MMRM analysis), LIBERTY as MEAN ± SE (MMRM secondary). The engine input 'sd' field is computed via sd = se * sqrt(n) using the per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows. _aact_se preserved as the raw SE for audit. Engine mdCovariance() uses sd^2/n which equals SE^2 — invariant to MEAN-vs-LSM labelling at the variance level.
se_to_sd_conversion: AACT-reported SE converted to SD via sd = se * sqrt(n) using the per-arm analyzed N from outcome_counts.txt Measure scope. This SD represents the within-arm SD of the MMRM-derived Δ MMD (NOT the residual model SD); for WLS engine use this is the correct quantity because variance(LSM) = SE^2 = SD^2 / N.
outcome_selection_rationale: Δ MMD (continuous, monthly migraine days change from baseline) chosen over the >=50% responder rate (binary) for 3 reasons: (a) Δ MMD is the registered PRIMARY endpoint in 2 of 3 trials (STRIVE, ARISE) and a registered SECONDARY in the third (LIBERTY); the binary >=50% responder rate is the registered PRIMARY only in LIBERTY (a Phase 3b in a refractory population) — so Δ MMD is the better cross-trial common endpoint. (b) Δ MMD preserves the magnitude of the migraine-day reduction (continuous information) versus collapsing the response distribution into a >=50% threshold cut. (c) Continuous endpoints are better-suited to dose-response slope estimation than dichotomized endpoints (more granular signal, no threshold-dependent slope distortion). The trade-off is that LIBERTY's MMD comes from the secondary register row not the primary — see estimand_uniformity above.
monotonicity_audit_2026_05_14: Per-trial Δ MMD dose-response monotonicity check (more negative Δ MMD at higher erenumab dose = greater reduction = treatment effect): (1) STRIVE: placebo -1.83 -> erenumab 70 mg -3.23 -> erenumab 140 mg -3.67 — STRICTLY MONOTONIC across 3 dose levels. (2) ARISE: placebo -1.84 -> erenumab 70 mg -2.88 — STRICTLY MONOTONIC (1 contrast). (3) LIBERTY: placebo -0.16 -> erenumab 140 mg -1.75 — STRICTLY MONOTONIC (1 contrast). Result: 4/4 contrasts strictly monotonic across all 3 trials. Within-trial dose-response signal is consistent and clinically interpretable; LIBERTY's small placebo response (-0.16 days) is the expected pattern in a 2-4 prior-preventive-failure treatment-refractory population.
pubmed_retrieval_notes: All 3 PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP (get_article_metadata + search_articles) on 2026-05-14. According to PubMed: STRIVE (PMID 29171821, doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1705848, N Engl J Med 2017;377(22):2123-2132, Goadsby PJ et al. — abstract confirms NCT02456740, n=955, Δ MMD -3.2/-3.7/-1.8 for 70mg/140mg/placebo at months 4-6, 50%-responder rate 43.3%/50.0%/26.6%); ARISE (PMID 29471679, doi:10.1177/0333102418759786, Cephalalgia 2018;38(6):1026-1037, Dodick DW et al. — abstract confirms NCT02483585, n=577, Δ MMD -2.9 vs -1.8 erenumab 70 mg vs placebo at month 3, 50%-responder rate 39.7% vs 29.5%); LIBERTY (PMID 30360965, doi:10.1016/S0140-6736(18)32534-0, Lancet 2018;392(10161):2280-2287, Reuter U et al. — abstract confirms NCT03096834, n=246, primary endpoint is 50%-responder rate 30% vs 14% at weeks 9-12, OR 2.7, Δ MMD secondary -1.75 vs -0.16, prior failure of 2-4 migraine preventives population). USER-CITATION ERRATUM: the user supplied 'LIBERTY: Reuter U et al. Lancet 2018;392(10161):2280-2287'. This bibliographic detail is correct, but PMID 30360966 (which a naive PMID guess from the same Lancet issue would land on) is a Lancet kinase-inhibition COMMENT article by McInnes/Siebert, NOT the LIBERTY paper. The correct LIBERTY PMID is 30360965, verified via PubMed search 'LIBERTY erenumab Reuter Lancet 2018' returning a unique hit with NCT03096834 in the abstract. Round 3.10 thus extends the citation-erratum pattern: Round 3.7 caught SUSTAIN-FORTE journal name, Round 3.9 caught AMAGINE-1 journal name, Round 3.10 caught LIBERTY PMID off-by-one.
data_provenance_summary: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN for STRIVE/ARISE primary; MEAN for LIBERTY secondary) + outcome_counts.txt (Measure scope, denominators). Outcome IDs: STRIVE=211326868 (NCT02456740 primary 'Change From Baseline in Mean Monthly Migraine Days to the Last 3 Months'), ARISE=211102571 (NCT02483585 primary 'Change From Baseline in Monthly Migraine Days at Week 12'), LIBERTY=211478876 (NCT03096834 secondary 'Change From Baseline in Monthly Migraine Days (MMD) in the Last Month'; LIBERTY's primary 211478875 is the binary >=50% responder rate). Cross-checked against Goadsby 2017 NEJM (STRIVE), Dodick 2018 Cephalalgia (ARISE), and Reuter 2018 Lancet (LIBERTY) published abstracts — all 4 within-trial dose-response contrasts (STRIVE pbo/70/140, ARISE pbo/70, LIBERTY pbo/140) match published values to 1 decimal place.

trials.background_therapy: no concomitant migraine preventive (washout before randomisation); acute abortive medications allowed per protocol
trials.population: Adults (18-65) with episodic migraine (4-14 MMD), no prior failure of >2 classes of preventives; baseline mean MMD 8.3 (across all arms); STRIVE was the pivotal Phase 3 trial supporting US FDA / EU EMA approval of erenumab for migraine prevention.
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211326868, registered PRIMARY 'Change From Baseline in Mean Monthly Migraine Days to the Last 3 Months of the Double-blind Treatment Period'). time_frame '4-week baseline phase and the last 3 months (months 4, 5, and 6) of the 24-week double-blind treatment phase'. param_type=LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN; dispersion_type='Standard Error' for all 3 result_groups (SE 0.18 in all 3 arms). Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows: 316 (placebo, OG000, rg 681977865), 312 (erenumab 70 mg QM, OG001, rg 681977866), 318 (erenumab 140 mg QM, OG002, rg 681977867). Per-arm LSM matches Goadsby NEJM 2017 abstract (Δ MMD: -1.8 placebo, -3.2 70 mg, -3.7 140 mg, all p<0.001 vs placebo at 2-dp AACT -1.83/-3.23/-3.67).

trials.background_therapy: no concomitant migraine preventive (washout before randomisation); acute abortive medications allowed
trials.population: Adults (18-65) with episodic migraine (4-14 MMD) without prior failure of multiple classes of preventives; n=577 randomized; ARISE was the 2nd pivotal Phase 3 placebo-controlled trial in the erenumab development programme; 70 mg only (no 140 mg arm).
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211102571, registered PRIMARY 'Change From Baseline in Monthly Migraine Days at Week 12'). time_frame '4-week baseline phase and the last 4 weeks of the 12-week double-blind treatment phase'. param_type=LEAST_SQUARES_MEAN; dispersion_type='Standard Error' for both result_groups (SE 0.21). Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows: 288 (placebo, OG000, rg 681233953), 282 (erenumab 70 mg QM, OG001, rg 681233954). Per-arm LSM matches Dodick Cephalalgia 2018 abstract (-2.88 erenumab vs -1.84 placebo; treatment difference -1.0 [95% CI -1.6, -0.5], p<0.001).

trials.background_therapy: no concomitant migraine preventive (washout before randomisation); acute abortive medications allowed. All patients had prior failure of 2-4 oral migraine preventive treatments (efficacy or tolerability or both).
trials.population: Adults (18-65) with episodic migraine (4-14 MMD) AND prior failure of 2-4 oral migraine preventive treatments (treatment-refractory). n=246 randomised (121 erenumab 140 mg, 125 placebo). 39% had failed 2 prior preventives, 38% had failed 3, 23% had failed 4. 12-week double-blind phase; 140 mg only (no 70 mg arm).
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211478876, registered SECONDARY 'Change From Baseline in Monthly Migraine Days (MMD) in the Last Month (Last 4 Weeks of Treatment)'; the registered PRIMARY for LIBERTY is the binary >=50% responder rate at week 12, outcome_id 211478875, 30.3% erenumab vs 13.7% placebo, OR 2.7). time_frame 'Baseline, Month 3 (last 4 weeks of treatment)'. param_type=MEAN (vs LSM for STRIVE/ARISE); dispersion_type='Standard Error' (SE 0.43 for erenumab 140 mg, SE 0.41 for placebo). Per-arm mITT N from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows: 120 (placebo, OG001, rg 682488283), 118 (erenumab 140 mg, OG000, rg 682488282). NOTE: AACT result_group ordering for LIBERTY is REVERSED vs STRIVE/ARISE — OG000 is erenumab 140 mg (not placebo), OG001 is placebo. Per-arm Δ MMD: erenumab 140 mg -1.75 (SE 0.43, n=118), placebo -0.16 (SE 0.41, n=120). Matches Reuter Lancet 2018 abstract (Δ MMD secondary): -1.61 [95% CI -2.70, -0.52] erenumab-vs-placebo difference, consistent with AACT contrast -1.75 - (-0.16) = -1.59 (1-dp).
